text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. During a code review, one developer checks another developer's code before it’s added to the main project. This practice is valuable for catching bugs early, when they are still easy and cheap to fix. Additionally, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team; less experienced developers learn new techniques, while senior developers gain insight into different parts of the codebase. Most importantly, regular code reviews help improve overall team quality. They encourage better communication, higher coding standards, and a shared sense of responsibility for the final product.
